config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Saya melihat kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi ia tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan kebebasan, martabat, dan keamanan tiap orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the greater good" can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since laws against violence, fraud, and public health harms show that some limits are necessary for everyone to live together safely. The key issue is not whether society matters, but whether any restriction on personal freedom is truly necessary, narrowly tailored, and accountable. If we lose that standard, "society" becomes a vague excuse to trample the very people it is supposed to serve.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus lebih dulu daripada hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik atau mengorbankan orang kecil. Memang benar hak individu tidak absolut, seperti soal keselamatan publik, penipuan, atau kekerasan, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, perlu, dan tidak berlebihan. Kalau tidak ada batas yang tegas, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan negara atau kelompok mayoritas yang makin leluasa menekan minoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika hak tiap orang dihormati terlebih dahulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun di atas fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government abuse. The point about public safety is real, but that does not mean society gets a blank check; restrictions should be limited, necessary, and tightly accountable, not vague appeals to the “greater good.” I also disagree with the idea that the collective should come first by default, because once you make individual rights secondary, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to pay the price. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals easily, but one that protects their rights so the whole system stays legitimate.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai pembenaran untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik, penipuan, atau kekerasan yang memang perlu pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dijadikan nomor dua, yang sering terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih baik, melainkan mayoritas atau penguasa yang makin leluasa bertindak semaunya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should come before the individual by default, because in the United States that usually ends up meaning the powerful get to define what counts as the public good. The repeated point about public safety is valid, but it only supports narrow limits, not a general rule that individual rights are secondary. In my view, rights are the foundation of a legitimate society, and when they are treated as optional, minorities and dissenters are the first to be sacrificed. A society is strongest when it can protect the common good without giving itself permission to override basic individual freedom whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
